title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing
text: outlines propose change package. detailed info contributing , packages, please see development contributing guide.

title: Contributing
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("tesselle/PACKAGE", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://documentation.tesselle.org.

title: Contributing
text: New code follow style guide. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing
text: Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Manual
text: arkhe package provides set S4 classes archaeological data matrices. new classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (taxon/type) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. Note missing values allowed. internal structure S4 classes implemented arkhe depicted UML class diagram following figure. UML class diagram S4 classes structure.

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) count matrix \(= \left[ a_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  a_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  \forall a_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: frequency matrix represents relative abundances. denote \(m \times p\) frequency matrix \(B = \left[ b_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  b_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} = 1 &&  b_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} b_{ij} &&  b_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} &&  \forall b_{ij} \\left[ 0,1 \right] \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: co-occurrence matrix symmetric matrix zeros main diagonal, works many times pairs taxa occur together least one sample. \(p \times p\) co-occurrence matrix \(D = \left[ d_{,j} \right] ~\forall ,j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) defined \(m \times p\) abundance matrix \(= \left[ a_{x,y} \right] ~\forall x \\left[ 1,m \right], y \\left[ 1,p \right]\) : \[ d_{,j} = \sum_{x = 1}^{m} \bigcap_{y = }^{j} a_{xy} \] row column sums: \[\begin{align}   d_{\cdot} = \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot j} = \sum_{\leqslant j}^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{p} \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   \forall d_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) incidence matrix \(C = \left[ c_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  c_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  \forall c_{ij} \\lbrace 0,1 \rbrace \end{align}\]

title: Manual
code: # Load packages library(arkhe)

title: Manual
text: new classes simple use, way base matrix:
code: set.seed(12345) ## Create a count data matrix ## Data are rounded to zero decimal places, then coerced with as.integer CountMatrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE),             nrow = 10, ncol = 10) #> <CountMatrix: 10 x 10> #>       col1 col2 col3 col4 col5 col6 col7 col8 col9 col10 #> row1     2    6    2    3    9    7    9    3    3     6 #> row2     9    9    8    7    9   10    6    8    9     0 #> row3     7    0    3   10    2    3    6   10    3     2 #> row4     9    7    9    5    2    1    4    0    8     1 #> row5    10    6    6    8    2    2    6    2    1     4 #> row6     7    5    1    4    0    5    9    9    7     9 #> row7     1    0    3    2    9    2    7    6    9     5 #> row8     5    3   10    0    7    6    2    9    0     6 #> row9    10    7    8    0   10    9    4    9    8     8 #> row10    5    9    8    4    8    6   10    6    5     9  ## Create an incidence (presence/absence) matrix ## Data are coerced to logical as by as.logical IncidenceMatrix(data = sample(0:1, 100, TRUE),                 nrow = 10, ncol = 10) #> <IncidenceMatrix: 10 x 10> #>        col1  col2  col3  col4  col5  col6  col7  col8  col9 col10 #> row1   TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE #> row2   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE #> row3   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row4   TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row5  FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row6   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE #> row7   TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row8  FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE #> row9  FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE #> row10  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE

title: Manual
text: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions:
code: ## Create a count matrix A0 <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ## Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(A0)  ## Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ## (use get_totals() to get these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ## Coerce to presence/absence C <- as_incidence(A1)  ## Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix D <- as_occurrence(A1)

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: collection classes represent archaeological data. package provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix (absolute/relative frequency, presence/absence data, co-occurrence matrix, etc.) upon package developers can build subclasses. also provides set generic methods (mutators coercion mechanisms) functions (e.g. predicates). addition, classes general interest (e.g. represent stratigraphic relationships) implemented.

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: can install released version arkhe CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("arkhe") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/arkhe")

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: arkhe provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency (compositional) data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (type/taxa) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. new classes simple use inherit base matrix: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes two special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area. coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign corresponding values. behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE).
code: ## Load the package library(arkhe) ## Define a count data matrix ## (data will be rounded to zero decimal places, then coerced with as.integer) quanti <- CountMatrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ## Define a logical matrix ## (data will be coerced with as.logical) quali <- IncidenceMatrix(data = sample(0:1, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10) ## Create a count matrix X <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 75, TRUE), nrow = 15, ncol = 5)  ## Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(X)  ## Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ## (use get_totals() to get these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ## Coerce to presence/absence D <- as_incidence(A1)  ## Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix E <- as_occurrence(A1) Y <- as.data.frame(X) Y$samples <- rep(c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"), each = 3) Y$groups <- rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 5)  ## Coerce to a count matrix Z <- as_count(Y)  ## Get groups get_samples(Z) #>  [1] "a" "a" "a" "b" "b" "b" "c" "c" "c" "d" "d" "d" "e" "e" "e"  ## Get groups get_groups(Z) #>  [1] "A" "A" "A" "A" "A" "B" "B" "B" "B" "B" "C" "C" "C" "C" "C"

title: Representation of Archaeological Data

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class

title: Stratigraphic Matrix — StratigraphicMatrix-class

what: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
title: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
text: collection classes represent     archaeological data. package provides set S4 classes     represent different special types matrix (absolute/relative     frequency, presence/absence data, co-occurrence matrix, etc.) upon     package developers can build subclasses. also provides set     generic methods (mutators coercion mechanisms) functions     (e.g. summary statistics, predicates). addition, classes      general interest (e.g. represent stratigraphic relationships)      implemented.

title: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
text: arkhe uses following options() configure behavior: arkhe.autodetect: logical scalar. Try automatically assign values corresponding slot *Matrix object coercing data.frame? Defaults TRUE. arkhe.verbose: logical scalar. R report extra information progress? Defaults TRUE.

code: as_long(from, ...)  as_count(from)  as_composition(from)  as_incidence(from)  as_occurrence(from)  as_features(from)  as_stratigraphy(from)  # S4 method for ANY as_count(from)  # S4 method for ANY as_composition(from)  # S4 method for ANY as_incidence(from)  # S4 method for ANY as_occurrence(from)  # S4 method for ANY as_stratigraphy(from)  # S4 method for matrix as_long(from, factor = FALSE, reverse = FALSE)  # S4 method for AbundanceMatrix as_long(from, factor = FALSE, reverse = FALSE)  # S4 method for AbundanceMatrix as_features(from)

text: object coerced.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: ... Currently used.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: factor logical scalar: character string coerced factor? Default FALSE, TRUE original ordering preserved.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: reverse logical scalar: order factor levels reversed? used factor TRUE. Useful plotting.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: coerced object.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: following methods coerce object *Matrix object: Note as_count rounds numeric values zero decimal places coerces integer .integer(). as_stratigraphy() converts set stratigraphic relationships (edges) stratigraphic (adjacency) matrix. can matrix, list, data.frame: first column/component assumed contain bottom units second top units (adjacency). as_features() converts *Matrix object collection features: data.frame informations extra columns (result may differ according class ).

title: Coerce — coerce
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: Check numeric objects: is_zero() checks object contains zeros. is_odd() is_even() check number odd even, respectively. is_positive() is_negative check object contains (strictly) positive negative numbers. is_whole() checks object contains whole numbers.

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
code: is_zero(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_odd(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_even(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_positive(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_negative(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_whole(x, na.rm = FALSE, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5)

text: x numeric object tested.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils

title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset

Character vectors matched name elements. empty index (comma separated blank) indicates entries dimension selected.
